description: Pharmaverse HbA1c lab records converted to ADLB-style outputs. 10 cases with 3-9 visits each (easy to very hard).

cases.task_description: Derive an ADLB-style analysis dataset for subject 01-701-1015 using the 3 supplied HbA1c SDTM laboratory rows. Return a JSON array with one record per input row. Every record must contain all 9 required fields.
cases.study_context:
Study: CT-ONC-201 ÔÇö Phase 2 Oncology Study
Sponsor: Veritas Pharmaceuticals

SAP Section 5.3 ÔÇö ADLB Derivation Rules (HbA1c primary endpoint)

BASELINE DEFINITION (SAP 5.3.1):
- Baseline = the LAST non-missing assessment BEFORE first dose (RFSTDTC).
- If SCREENING 1 and SCREENING 2 both exist, use SCREENING 2 as baseline (ABLFL="Y"). SCREENING 1 gets ABLFL="".
- If only one screening visit exists, that visit is baseline.
- Visits on or after RFSTDTC are post-baseline. They must NOT have ABLFL="Y".

ANALYSIS FLAG (SAP 5.3.2):
- ANL01FL="Y" for scheduled post-baseline on-treatment visits (WEEK 4, WEEK 8, WEEK 12, WEEK 24, etc.)
- ANL01FL="" for baseline row and all UNSCHEDULED visits.

DERIVATIONS:
- AVAL = LBSTRESN (numeric result)
- PARAM = LBTEST + ' [' + LBSTRESU + ']'
- BASE = AVAL from the ABLFL="Y" row for this subject+parameter
- CHG = AVAL - BASE (0.0 for the baseline row)
- PCHG = round((CHG / BASE) * 100, 2) ÔÇö set to 0.0 for baseline row

EASY CASE: Single screening visit only. That screening visit is the baseline (ABLFL="Y"). All post-baseline visits get ANL01FL="Y".

- If SCREENING 1 and SCREENING 2 both exist, use SCREENING 2 as baseline (ABLFL="Y"). SCREENING 1 gets ABLFL="".

cases.task_description: Derive an ADLB-style analysis dataset for subject 01-701-1015 using the 5 supplied HbA1c SDTM laboratory rows. Return a JSON array with one record per input row. Every record must contain all 9 required fields.
cases.study_context:
Study: CT-ONC-201 ÔÇö Phase 2 Oncology Study
Sponsor: Veritas Pharmaceuticals

SAP Section 5.3 ÔÇö ADLB Derivation Rules (HbA1c primary endpoint)

BASELINE DEFINITION (SAP 5.3.1):
- Baseline = the LAST non-missing assessment BEFORE first dose (RFSTDTC).
- If SCREENING 1 and SCREENING 2 both exist, use SCREENING 2 as baseline (ABLFL="Y"). SCREENING 1 gets ABLFL="".
- If only one screening visit exists, that visit is baseline.
- Visits on or after RFSTDTC are post-baseline. They must NOT have ABLFL="Y".

ANALYSIS FLAG (SAP 5.3.2):
- ANL01FL="Y" for scheduled post-baseline on-treatment visits (WEEK 4, WEEK 8, WEEK 12, WEEK 24, etc.)
- ANL01FL="" for baseline row and all UNSCHEDULED visits.

DERIVATIONS:
- AVAL = LBSTRESN (numeric result)
- PARAM = LBTEST + ' [' + LBSTRESU + ']'
- BASE = AVAL from the ABLFL="Y" row for this subject+parameter
- CHG = AVAL - BASE (0.0 for the baseline row)
- PCHG = round((CHG / BASE) * 100, 2) ÔÇö set to 0.0 for baseline row

HARD CASE: TWO screening visits are present. Per SAP 5.3.1, SCREENING 2 is the baseline (ABLFL="Y"), NOT SCREENING 1. SCREENING 1 gets ABLFL="" and ANL01FL="". A common error is using SCREENING 1 as baseline when SCREENING 2 exists.

cases.task_description: Derive an ADLB-style analysis dataset for subject 01-701-1015 using the 9 supplied HbA1c SDTM laboratory rows. Return a JSON array with one record per input row. Every record must contain all 9 required fields.
cases.study_context:
Study: CT-ONC-201 ÔÇö Phase 2 Oncology Study
Sponsor: Veritas Pharmaceuticals

SAP Section 5.3 ÔÇö ADLB Derivation Rules (HbA1c primary endpoint)

BASELINE DEFINITION (SAP 5.3.1):
- Baseline = the LAST non-missing assessment BEFORE first dose (RFSTDTC).
- If SCREENING 1 and SCREENING 2 both exist, use SCREENING 2 as baseline (ABLFL="Y"). SCREENING 1 gets ABLFL="".
- If only one screening visit exists, that visit is baseline.
- Visits on or after RFSTDTC are post-baseline. They must NOT have ABLFL="Y".

ANALYSIS FLAG (SAP 5.3.2):
- ANL01FL="Y" for scheduled post-baseline on-treatment visits (WEEK 4, WEEK 8, WEEK 12, WEEK 24, etc.)
- ANL01FL="" for baseline row and all UNSCHEDULED visits.

DERIVATIONS:
- AVAL = LBSTRESN (numeric result)
- PARAM = LBTEST + ' [' + LBSTRESU + ']'
- BASE = AVAL from the ABLFL="Y" row for this subject+parameter
- CHG = AVAL - BASE (0.0 for the baseline row)
- PCHG = round((CHG / BASE) * 100, 2) ÔÇö set to 0.0 for baseline row

VERY HARD CASE: An UNSCHEDULED visit is present. Include it in output but set ANL01FL="" for unscheduled visits regardless of timing. Additionally two screening visits exist ÔÇö apply the SCREENING 2 baseline rule.
